assumptions: No Rollbar account, project, SDK choice, access token, instrumented application, captured occurrence, or dashboard item is assumed.
A browser, working identity, application codebase, development environment, and network connection are available.
No signup method was authorized or submitted, account or project was created, token was viewed or generated, code was changed, or occurrence was sent during research.

developer_goal: Start without a Rollbar account or project, choose the SDK matching an application, instrument it with the correct project access token, send a documented test occurrence, and verify that the occurrence appears in Rollbar.

first_success.outcome: The selected Rollbar SDK sends a test error or message from the instrumented application and the resulting item becomes visible in the Rollbar dashboard.
first_success.observable_signal: The simulated uncaught error appears in the project's Rollbar dashboard within a few seconds.

route_selection.surface: Rollbar SDK selection and first captured occurrence
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Rollbar SDK selection and first captured occurrence
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Browser JavaScript quick start
candidates.reason: This provides a fully described browser-specific example but the general overview lists it alongside other language SDKs, and even the page recommends module imports for most modern web applications.

candidates.name: Node.js SDK
candidates.reason: This is a server-side JavaScript route requiring a different project token and code integration.

candidates.name: Rollbar REST API
candidates.reason: The general overview identifies SDK instrumentation as the first step rather than selecting a direct API route.

required_path.action: Complete Rollbar account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Rollbar account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Rollbar candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The simulated uncaught error appears in the project's Rollbar dashboard within a few seconds.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'identity', 'requirement': 'Create a Rollbar account using GitHub, Google, or email signup.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'Create or select a Rollbar project for the application being instrumented.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S3', 'S4']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'knowledge', 'requirement': "Choose the SDK and integration route matching the application's language and framework.", 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}

external_gates.description: A Rollbar account and project are required before obtaining a project token and seeing captured occurrences.
The public signup page does not expose the exact post-submission account and project states.
The developer must choose an SDK and often a framework-specific integration.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

excluded.item: Source maps, deploy tracking, person tracking, notifications, issue integrations, telemetry enrichment, additional environments, and production-quality error handling occur after the first captured oc
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

sources.title: Overview
evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

sources.title: Rollbar Signup
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Rollbar Signup

sources.title: Browser JS
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Browser JS

sources.title: Access Tokens
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Access Tokens

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Rollbar show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
